properties.remark: POLICE OFFICER REPORTED LARGE HAIL ON US ROUTE 1 IN THE TOWN OF BAILEYVILLE. TIME OF THE REPORT IS ESTIMATED.
TREES DOWN ON ROUTE 11.
TREES BLOW DOWN ACROSS THE SHIN POND ROAD.
CHURCH STREET FLOODED. VEHICLE STRANDED.
WATER FLOWING INTO HOUSES ON MAIN ROAD AND CHURCH STREET. ONE PERSON STRANDED IN THEIR HOUSE WHICH IS SUROUNDED BY WATER.
TOWN OF BROWNVILLE ALMOST COMPLETELY CUT OFF...MOST MAJOR ROADS CLOSED...RTE 11...CHURCH ST...PLEASANT RIVER RD CLOSED WITH WASHOUTS.
MINOR ST FLOODING...BASEMENT ARE BEING FLOODED FIRE DEPARMENT HAS BEEN OUT DRAINING WATER FROM BASEMENTS AND ATTEMPTING TO KEEP ROADS OPEN.
WATERS RD IN PATTEN HAS WATER FLOWING OVER IT...RTE 11 NORTH OF PATTEN FLOODED.

SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE HAS BEEN REPORTED ON MANY ROADS AROUND BROWNVILLE. THESE INCLUDE CHURCH ST...HIGH ST...RTE 11...PLEASANT RIVER RD...QUARRY RD...BARNARD RD...AND STICKNEY HILL RD. ALL THESE ROADS HAVE EITHER BEEN HEAVILY DAMAGED OR ENTIRELY WASHED OUT. SEVERAL BRIDGES HAVE ALSO BEEN SIGNIFICANTLY DAMAGED OR WASHED AWAY. BROWNVILLE IS LARGEY ISOLATED WITH MOST ROADS HEAVILY DAMAGED OR IMPASSABLE. POWER IS OUT IN THE TOWN. SOME RESCUES ARE ONGOING.
ROUTE 11 FLOODED BUT HAS BEEN OPENED TO TRAFFIC.
SOME ROADS IN MILLINOCKET WERE FLOODED.
CLARK ROAD HAS BEEN WASHED OUT WITH BRIDGE DAMAGE. THE ROAD HAS BEEN CLOSED TO TRAFFIC.
ROADS FLOODED DUE TO VERY HEAVY RAINFALL.
*** 1 FATAL *** ONE INDIVIDUAL REPORTEDLY KILLED IN FLASH FLOODING ON PLEASANT RIVER RD IN BROWNVILLE OVERNIGHT. PREVIOUSLY REPORTED.
*** 1 FATAL *** ONE INDIVIDUAL REPORTEDLY KILLED IN FLASH FLOODING ON PLEASANT STREET IN BROWNVILLE OVERNIGHT. TIME UNKNOWN.
SOME ROADS AROUND THE AMITY AREA HAVE WATER ACROSS THEM WITH CULVERTS BEING WASHED OUT.
PEIRCE BROOK OVERFLOWING ITS BANKS IN HODGDON.
100 YARDS OF LYCETTE ROAD IN THE TOWN OF AMITY WAS WASHED OUT. THE ROAD HAS BEEN CLOSED.
*** 1 FATAL *** ONE INDIVIDUAL REPORTEDLY KILLED IN FLASH FLOODING ON PLEASANT RIVER RD IN BROWNVILLE OVERNIGHT. PREVIOUSLY REPORTED.
SEVERAL LARGE TREES DOWN
QUARTER SIZE HAIL PERSISTED FOR ABOUT 20 MINUTES.
GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL IN CONNOR
SCATTERED TREE DAMAGE THROUGHOUT CARIBOU.
1 1/2 HAIL
MADAWASKA ROAD FLOODED IN SPOTS.
NUMEROUS ROADS WASHED OUT.

MADAWASKA ROAD FLOODED.
REPORT OF TREES DOWN ON POWERLINES

EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE AT AND WITHIN A MILE OF AROOSTOOK VALLEY COUNTRY CLUB. DOZENS OF TREES DOWNED, MANY BEING LARGE AND OLD. WINDS ESTIMATED 60-70 MPH RESULTING FROM STRAIGHTLINE DOWNBURST WINDS. SURVEYED BY NWS EMPLOYEE.

TREES DOWN ON THE GRIMES ROAD IN FORT FAIRFIELD. TIME IS ESTIMATED.

TREE DOWN ON BROWNSVILLE RD. WIND GUST REPORTED AT MLT ASOS AT 54 MPH AT THIS TIME WELL AFTER TERMINATION OF LIGHTNING WITH A WEAKENING LINE OF SHOWERS.
